behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser/segmentation logic itself. Behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess, model output, or cross-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the segmenter with a multi-line shell snippet and assert it returns distinct statement segments per newline-separated command/assignment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module handoff: segmentation output must be consumed correctly by trigger classification. Rejected System because behavior text only describes parsing/classification, not real subprocess execution (`gh`) or network.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test wiring segmenter + trigger detector: feed a multiline block (`VAR=...` lines then `gh pr create ...`) and assert final classification is PR-create trigger, not `not_a_trigger`.

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Also a boundary between segmentation and command-intent classification, but negative case. Rejected higher levels because behavior text has no real infra dependency and no AI/model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with multiline assignments + `gh issue create`; assert detector returns non-PR classification and specifically does not open PR-create path.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: Needs multiple local components (trigger result -> identifier reconstruction -> state persistence) and filesystem write/read contract. Rejected System: behavior text requires persistence, not real external subprocess/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that supplies detected PR-create context, runs identifier reconstruction + persistence, then verifies stored state contains expected PR identifier fields for downstream use.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-module state handoff (post-hook writer to pre-hook reader/enforcer) via local state format. Rejected System because behavior text focuses schema/contract compatibility, not OS/network/subprocess-specific failure.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test across both hook modules: run post-hook write path, then invoke pre-hook read/enforce path against that state and assert enforcement activates correctly.
